content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                                    Date : June 27, 1980

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Sarah Cameron

Subject: Letter from Jack Ortega


        I called Mr. Ortega today and apologized for the fact that
we had not responded earlier to his letter.  I explained that it
had become lost in our procedures somewhere.

        I told him that cannot provide advice to third parties and
therefore cannot answer his letter as advice.  I asked him if he
would like a package of materials from the Enforcement Division
explaining how to file a complaint, and he said, "Yes."  I therefore
gave his name and address to Enforcement.

        I did explain also that the Act in general only addresses
conflicts of interest arising from financial interests held by an
official, and I told him what Section 87103 said.  He asked if
reimbursements from his organization would be "income."  I asked
if they were a bona fide charitable organization, eligible to have
contributions to them be deductible.  He said,"Yes," and I told
him that reimbursements for per diem and travel expenses were
therefore not income under the provisions of Section 82030.

CALEXICO INDUSTRIAL PARK

DIRECTOR JACK ORTEGA • P. O. BOX 789 • CALEXICO, CALIFORNIA 92231 • PHONE (714) 357-6464

February 5, 1980


Political Practices Commissioner
Conflict of Interest Division
1100 K St. Building
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Sirs:

The Chairman of the City of Calexico Planning Commission, Oscar Gonzales, is an expelled, ex-member of this non-profit corporation, the Calexico Community Action Council, Inc., (C.C.A.C.), a community development corporation who project is the development of a 66 acre industrial park. This chairman has expressed through public statement and letters a bias and contempt for our $35,000.00 economic development effort. Over $3,000,000.00 of public funds have been invested in this project.

His harrassments have included an unauthorized, Sunday entry onto our site located offices to copy records, using a federally funded copying machine and employee for political activity against the project. There is no way we can receive a fair and objective decision on any matter dealing with the Calexico Industrial Park from him.

In the coming year we will have numerous appearances before this body of decision makers and feel that he will attempt to discourage potential investors with obstacles and ruses. We have asked that he disqualify himself on matters dealing with this industrial park, but he has refused. Please advise us on this situation.

Sincerely yours,

Jack Ortega
Director

JO/nc

There is no way we can receive a fair and objective decision on any matter dealing with the Calexico Industrial Park from him. In the coming year we will have numerous appearances before this body of decision makers and feel that he will attempt to discourage potential investors with obstacles and ruses. We have asked that he disqualify himself on matters dealing with this industrial park, but he has refused. Please advise us on this situation.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Chairman of the Calexico Planning Commission should recuse himself from decisions concerning an industrial park project due to his history with and stated bias against the inquirer's non-profit organization that is developing the project.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is primarily an incoming request/complaint letter with an attached internal FPPC memo documenting the agency's response. The memo indicates FPPC declined to provide formal advice to the third party (Ortega) but provided limited guidance on conflict of interest law and directed him to the Enforcement Division for complaint filing procedures.

embedding.summary: This document contains an incoming complaint letter from Jack Ortega, Director of the Calexico Community Action Council, regarding alleged bias and conflict of interest by Planning Commission Chairman Oscar Gonzales, along with a June 1980 FPPC internal memorandum documenting Sarah Cameron's phone conversation with Ortega explaining FPPC's limited authority to advise third parties.
